reasoning: The most recent value of the ICE BofA AA US Corporate Index effective yield was 4.38% on October 24, 2025, which lies at the lower end of its October range (falling from a mid-month high of ~4.52%). Short-term mean reversion suggests a reasonably high likelihood of an increase above 4.38% over the next week or two, which aligns with the FRED tool's short-term combined threshold exceedance probability of ~0.62. However, stretching out into late November and January 2026, the potential for further Federal Reserve rate cuts establishes stronger downward pressure on broader corporate yields, reducing the likelihood of sustained increases. Following the requirement to increase uncertainty (bringing probabilities closer to 0.5 with longer horizons) and given the broader macro downtrend, the forecast tapers from a modestly positive chance of an increase in early November down to a near coin-toss for late January 2026.
